하박국 3:1.content: A prayer of Habakkuk, the prophet, set to victorious music.

하박국 3:2.content: Yahweh, I have heard of your fame. I stand in awe of your deeds, Yahweh. Renew your work in the middle of the years. In the middle of the years make it known. In wrath, you remember mercy.

하박국 3:3.content: God came from Teman, the Holy One from Mount Paran. His glory covered the heavens, and his praise filled the earth.

하박국 3:4.content: His splendor is like the sunrise. Rays shine from his hand, where his power is hidden.

하박국 3:5.content: Plague went before him, and pestilence followed his feet.

하박국 3:6.content: He stood, and shook the earth. He looked, and made the nations tremble. The ancient mountains were crumbled. The age-old hills collapsed. His ways are eternal.

하박국 3:7.content: I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction. The dwellings of the land of Midian trembled.

하박국 3:8.content: Was Yahweh displeased with the rivers? Was your anger against the rivers, or your wrath against the sea, that you rode on your horses, on your chariots of salvation?

하박국 3:9.content: You uncovered your bow. You called for your sworn arrows. You split the earth with rivers.

하박국 3:10.content: The mountains saw you, and were afraid. The storm of waters passed by. The deep roared and lifted up its hands on high.

하박국 3:11.content: The sun and moon stood still in the sky, at the light of your arrows as they went, at the shining of your glittering spear.

하박국 3:12.content: You marched through the land in wrath. You threshed the nations in anger.

하박국 3:13.content: You went out for the salvation of your people, for the salvation of your anointed. You crushed the head of the land of wickedness. You stripped them head to foot.

하박국 3:14.content: You pierced the heads of his warriors with their own spears. They came as a whirlwind to scatter me, gloating as if to devour the wretched in secret.

하박국 3:15.content: You trampled the sea with your horses, churning mighty waters.

하박국 3:16.content: I heard, and my body trembled. My lips quivered at the voice. Rottenness enters into my bones, and I tremble in my place, because I must wait quietly for the day of trouble, for the coming up of the people who invade us.

하박국 3:17.content: For though the fig tree doesn’t flourish, nor fruit be in the vines; the labor of the olive fails, the fields yield no food; the flocks are cut off from the fold, and there is no herd in the stalls:

하박국 3:18.content: yet I will rejoice in Yahweh. I will be joyful in the God of my salvation!

하박국 3:19.content: Yahweh, the Lord, is my strength. He makes my feet like deer’s feet, and enables me to go in high places. For the music director, on my stringed instruments.
